homepage.h1: Proudly Representing Sheffield South East since 1992
homepage.meta_description: Official website of Clive Betts MP, serving Sheffield South East.

homepage.text_excerpt: Clive Betts MP - Sheffield South East Representation Clive Betts MP Home Proudly Representing Sheffield South East since 1992 We get a large amount of correspondence ranging from Constituents personal problems to important political matters of the day and everything in between. We do our upmost to respond to everyone in a timely manner but if you have reached out to me regarding a pollical or campaign issue you may find the response on the pages to the right. If you are writing to me with regards to one these issues please make sure we are able to reply directly to the email. pages.title: Blog
headings: h3: Your web browser is old and not fully supported.
h3: For a better experience, please use a modern browser:
pages.text_excerpt: Blog Clive Betts MP Home WASPI Campaign I have been receiving a lot emails from Constituents regarding the WASPI campaign I do understand the disappointment felt by those effected by this issue and by past announcements by previous Governments and the new Government that they do not intend to compensate Waspi women and that they the rejected the Ombudsman’s findings. Although I can see that awarding full compensation is probably not realistic given then overall cost to the Treasury and the precedent it would potentially set on other related retirement age issues, I am surprised that the Government has not agreed so far to some partial compensation given the Ombudsman’s report is very clear that the Department for Work and Pensions did not communicate changes to the Pension age clearly. Ministers have indicated in the past week that will be making announcement on this in early March. I have previously to Ministers to ask if they would reconsider the issue and the response I received can be found here: https://acrobat.adobe.com/id/urn:aaid:sc:EU:79e6a1bd-b092-415a-aaf0-2555b2a9acc5 I will write to Ministers again to raise the issues and I will post the response here as soon as it is received. Update 27/02/26 - Please find the response from the Minister here: https://acrobat.adobe.com/id/urn:aaid:sc:EU:c24f8254-8556-4616-a941-ad89c7770233 ©Copyright. All rights reserved.
